questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. One who does something for pleasure.
questions.explanation: B)Hedonist(noun) – One who does something for pleasure or pursues pleasure as the highest good. आनंदवादी Atheist (noun)– A person who does not believe in the existence of God. नास्तिक Anarchist(noun) – A person who believes in or tries to bring about anarchy (a state of disorder due to absence of authority). अराजकतावादी Aggressor (noun)– A person or country that attacks first or initiates hostility. आक्रमणकारी

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: D) The correct spelling isPerseverance, which means "persistence in doing something despite difficulty or delay in achieving success"."कठिनाइयों या देरी के बावजूद किसी कार्य में लगे रहना"।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select No improvement. Lot of peoples visit this restaurant as its biryani is famous.
questions.explanation: C)(Lot of peoples)’के बदले ‘A lot of people’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि ‘people’ एक ऐसा शब्द है जिसे हम बहुवचन में ही प्रयोग करते हैं और ‘a lot of’ का प्रयोग इसके साथ होता है। जैसे — A lot of people came to see the match.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. The place where soldiers live.
questions.explanation: A)Barracks(noun) – Buildings used to house soldiers सैनिकों के रहने का स्थान Unit(noun) – A small group of military personnel इकाई Regiment(noun) – A military unit of ground forces consisting of two or more battalions रेजिमेंट Brigade(noun) – A military unit consisting of several regiments or battalions ब्रिगेड

questions.question: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order. A. Although I had a fear of water, I thought it was an important skill I should learn. B. However, what I didn't realize was that it would also make me a more confident person. C. I also thought it would be a good exercise that would make me physically stronger. D. One of the hardest things I've had to do was to learn how to swim.
questions.explanation: C)DACB D starts the sentence with the subject "One of the hardest things I've had to do", introducing the main topic (learning to swim).A follows D because it provides additional context about the subject’s fear of water but also their motivation to learn swimming."Although" contrasts the fear (in D) with the importance of learning (A)C comes next as it continues the reasoning with "I also thought", adding another benefit (physical exercise).: "Also" links it to the previous idea in A, expanding on why the subject wanted to learnB concludes the sentence by introducing a new realization (confidence) with "However, what I didn't realize", contrasting with the earlier thoughts. "However" contrasts the expected benefits (A, C) with the unexpected outcome (B). D पहले आता है क्योंकि यह subject "One of the hardest things" को introduce करता है, जो main topic (swimming सीखना) को set करता है। A, D के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह "Although" से शुरू होकर subject के डर और motivation को जोड़ता है। C, A के बाद आता है क्योंकि "I also thought" पिछली reasoning को आगे बढ़ाता है (physical strength का benefit)। B End में आता है क्योंकि "However" एक नए perspective (confidence) को introduce करता है, जो पहले के विचारों के साथ contrast करता है।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Following weeks of intense training, the athlete ____________________ in the championship.
questions.explanation: D) ‘Competed'का use होगा क्योंकि 'competed' का अर्थ है प्रतिस्पर्धा करना। sentence में mention है कि एथलीट ने गहन प्रशिक्षण के बाद चैंपियनशिप में भाग लिया, जो एक completed action है। वाक्य past tense में है, इसलिए 'competed' सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प है। A. 'have been competing' का अर्थ है कि एथलीट अभी भी प्रतिस्पर्धा कर रहा है, जो वाक्य के संदर्भ से मेल नहीं खाता। B. 'were competing' का अर्थ है कि एथलीट किसी समय प्रतिस्पर्धा कर रहे थे, लेकिन वाक्य भूतकाल में पूर्ण क्रिया की ओर इशारा करता है।'have competed' present perfect tense में है, लेकिन यहाँ simple past tense action चाहिए। 'D.Competed'will be used because 'competed' means to have participated in a competition. The sentence indicates that the athlete participated in the championship after weeks of intense training, which is a completed action. The sentence is in the past tense, making 'competed' the most appropriate choice. 'have been competing' implies that the athlete is still competing, which doesn’t align with the context of the sentence. 'were competing' suggests an ongoing action in the past, but the sentence refers to a completed action in the past. 'have competed' is in the present perfect tense, but the sentence requires a simple past tense action.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom from the options given below. To accept the gauntlet
questions.explanation: C)To accept the gauntlet(idiom) – This means to accept a challenge चुनौती स्वीकार करना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) had _________ that the city would witness rainfall on Thursday.
questions.explanation: A)'Forecast'का use सही है क्योंकि इसका अर्थ है भविष्यवाणी करना या पहले से अनुमान लगाना। वाक्य में बताया गया है कि भारतीय मौसम विभाग (IMD) ने गुरुवार को शहर में बारिश होने की संभावना के बारे में पूर्वानुमान दिया था। इसलिए 'forecast' यहाँ सही विकल्प है। Manifest का अर्थ है स्पष्ट रूप से दिखाना या प्रकट करना, जो इस संदर्भ में फिट नहीं होता। Contest का अर्थ है मुकाबला करना या विवाद करना, जो इस वाक्य के अर्थ से मेल नहीं खाता। Prolong का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ को लंबा खींचना, जो इस वाक्य के संदर्भ में सही नहीं है। Forecast'is the correct answer because it means to predict or estimate something in advance. The sentence indicates that the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) predicted that the city would witness rainfall on Thursday, making 'forecast' the suitable choice. 'Manifest' means to show or display clearly, which doesn’t fit the context here. 'Contest' means to challenge or dispute, which is irrelevant to the sentence's meaning. 'Prolong' means to extend something in time, which doesn’t match the context either.

questions.question: In the sentence identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. Three more elevated roads have been propose in the new plan to unclog the traffic in Delhi.
options: to unclog the traffic in Delhi
questions.explanation: D) 'have been propose'के बदले 'have been proposed' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि Passive Voice में Third form of Verb का इस्तेमाल होता है; जैसे— The plan has been approved by the committee

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given italicize word. The arena was packed with spectators, all eagerly waiting for the start of the championship match.
questions.explanation: D) Arena(noun) – field, domain, sphere, platform, stage क्षेत्र Synonym: Domain(noun) – An area of expertise or activity. (क्षेत्र) Court(noun) – A place for legal proceedings. (अदालत) House(noun) – A building or residence. (घर) Office(noun) – A place of work. (कार्यालय)

questions.question: Select the correct spelling of the incorrectly spelt word in the given sentence. She is an indepandent writer.
questions.explanation: B) The correct spelling of the incorrectly spelt word is ‘Independent’, which means “free from outside control; not depending on another's authority”. स्वतंत्र, जो दूसरों पर निर्भर नहीं हो।

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. The analysis of a person's handwriting to determine his/her personality.
questions.explanation: D)Graphology(noun) – The analysis of a person's handwriting to determine his/her personality. हस्तलेख विज्ञान Calligraphy(noun) – The art of beautiful handwriting. सुंदर लिखावट की कला Numismatics(noun) – The study or collection of coins, banknotes, and medals. सिक्के, नोट, और पदकों का अध्ययन या संग्रह Philology(noun) – The study of language in written historical sources; linguistics. भाषा और उसके साहित्य का अध्ययन

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given italicize word. The relevance of this data to our current project is undeniable, especially when considering market trends.
questions.explanation: C) Relevance(noun) – importance, significance, pertinence, applicability, value प्रासंगिकता Synonym: Significance(noun) – Importance or meaning (महत्व) Irrelevance(noun) – Lack of importance (अप्रासंगिकता) Triviality(noun) – Something of little importance (तुच्छता) Inconsequence(noun) – Lack of importance (निरर्थकता)

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given italicize word. The teacher warned the students to heed her instructions before starting the experiment.
questions.explanation: A)Heed(verb) – Pay attention, listen to, consider, take notice, obey. ध्यान देनाAntonym:Ignore(verb) – To deliberately pay no attention to something, disregard. अनदेखा करना•Consider(verb) – To think about carefully, deliberate. विचार करना•Obey(verb) – To comply with, follow the rules or commands. पालन करना•Notice(verb) – To observe or become aware of something. ध्यान देना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select No improvement. Nowadays rent for a two-room house can run as high to Rs. 40,000/ in Mumbai.
questions.explanation: D) 'as high to'के बदले 'as high as' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि 'as high as' सही तरीका है मूल्य की तुलना करने के लिए। जैसे— The price can go as high as Rs. 1,00,000.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given italicize word. Water is a vital resource for sustaining all forms of life on Earth.
questions.explanation: D)Vital(adjective) – Essential, crucial, key, important, necessary. अत्यावश्यक Antonym:Unimportant(adjective) – Not significant, of little value. महत्वहीन •Crucial(adjective) – Extremely important or necessary. महत्वपूर्ण •Necessary(adjective) – Required or needed. आवश्यक •Essential(adjective) – Life-sustaining, essential. अत्यावश्यक

questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom that can substitute the highlighted text in the given sentence. The minister was in partnership with the queen in poisoning the King.
questions.explanation: A) in cahoots(idiom) – in a secret partnership (गुप्त साठ-गाँठ) B) at one's beck and call– always ready to obey someone’s orders (किसी के इशारे पर तैयार रहना) C) run amok– to behave uncontrollably and violently (उन्मत्त होकर हिंसक व्यवहार करना) D) in the blues– feeling sad or depressed (उदास होना)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select ‘No substitution’. Suresh has only been working here since one month and he has already learnt the job.
questions.explanation: A) 'here since one month'के बदले 'here for one month' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि 'since' का प्रयोग specific point in time के लिए होता है, जबकि 'for' का प्रयोग duration of time के लिए होता है; जैसे— He has been waiting here for two hours. 'here for one month' will be used instead of 'here since one month' because 'since' is used for a specific point in time, while 'for' is used for a duration of time; Like— He has been waiting here for two hours.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select option ‘No substitution required’. Often it makes one wonder at the sights you see.
questions.explanation: A) ‘you see'के बदले 'one sees' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि subject 'one' के साथ Verb का singular form 'sees' आएगा; जैसे— One must do one’s duty. one sees' will be used instead of 'you see' because the subject 'one' requires the verb to be in singular form 'sees'; Like— One must do one’s duty.

questions.question: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Out of the given options pick the one that gives their correct order. A) He is a gifted volleyball player. B) But now a days he does not play international matches. C) It is because he had an accident last year. D) Sanjay is my best friend.
questions.explanation: B) DABC D introduces the subject "Sanjay." A describes Sanjay's ability (subject + verb + complement). B contrasts A with "But nowadays" (adverbial phrase + subject + verb). C explains B with "It is because" (cause-effect connector). D sentence की शुरुआत करता है क्योंकि यह subject "Sanjay" को introduce करता है, जिसके बारे में बात की जा रही है। A, D के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह Sanjay के बारे में additional information देता है (उसकी volleyball skills)। B, A के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह "But nowadays" से contrast बनाता है, बताता है कि वह अब international matches नहीं खेलता। C, B का reason देता है ("It is because") कि क्यों वह नहीं खेलता (last year accident हुआ था)।

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The southwest monsoon stormed into Kerala, a week ahead of its scheduled arrival on June 1. This was not a surprise because as early as the first week of May, meteorologists had ____A_____ the country about its early arrival over the Andaman Sea — a crucial pit stop before its _____B_______ over Kerala. A May onset is not an exceptional occurrence — it has happened four times _______C______2014 — but additional support from a pre-cyclonic circulation in the Arabian Sea gave it a _______D_______ surge due to which several parts of Maharashtra and the Konkan coast saw the monsoon reach their shores earlier than anticipated. It was the first time in 35 years that Mumbai, which usually welcomes the monsoon around June 10, saw a May onset. Even prior to the onset, the India Meteorological Department (IMD), as far back as mid-April, had forecast an ‘above normal’ monsoon rainfall, or 5% ______E______ the historical average of 87 cm. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1
questions.explanation: A) 'Tipped off' का use होगा क्योंकि "tipped off" का अर्थ होता है किसी को पहले से सूचना या चेतावनी देना। passage में mention किया गया है कि मई के पहले हफ्ते में ही मौसम वैज्ञानिकों ने देश को इसके जल्दी आने के बारे में सूचित कर दिया था, इसलिए 'tipped off' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'taper off' का अर्थ है धीरे-धीरे कम होना, 'peter out' का अर्थ है समाप्त हो जाना, और 'kicked off' का अर्थ है शुरू करना, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'Tipped off' will be used because it means to inform or warn someone in advance. The passage mentions that meteorologists had informed the country about its early arrival as early as the first week of May, making 'tipped off' fitting here. Whereas, 'taper off' means to gradually decrease, 'peter out' means to fade away, and 'kicked off' means to start, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The southwest monsoon stormed into Kerala, a week ahead of its scheduled arrival on June 1. This was not a surprise because as early as the first week of May, meteorologists had ____A_____ the country about its early arrival over the Andaman Sea — a crucial pit stop before its _____B_______ over Kerala. A May onset is not an exceptional occurrence — it has happened four times _______C______2014 — but additional support from a pre-cyclonic circulation in the Arabian Sea gave it a _______D_______ surge due to which several parts of Maharashtra and the Konkan coast saw the monsoon reach their shores earlier than anticipated. It was the first time in 35 years that Mumbai, which usually welcomes the monsoon around June 10, saw a May onset. Even prior to the onset, the India Meteorological Department (IMD), as far back as mid-April, had forecast an ‘above normal’ monsoon rainfall, or 5% ______E______ the historical average of 87 cm. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2
questions.explanation: D) 'Advent' का use होगा क्योंकि "advent" का अर्थ होता है किसी महत्वपूर्ण घटना या चीज़ का आगमन। passage में mention किया गया है कि मानसून केरल के ऊपर पहुँचने से पहले अंडमान सागर में एक crucial pit stop बनाता है, इसलिए 'advent' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Discernment' का अर्थ है समझ या विवेक, 'Opulent' का अर्थ है धनी या विलासी, और 'Affluent' का अर्थ है समृद्ध, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'Advent' will be used because it means the arrival of an important event or thing. The passage mentions that the monsoon makes a crucial pit stop over the Andaman Sea before its arrival over Kerala, making 'advent' fitting here. Whereas, 'Discernment' means understanding or judgment, 'Opulent' means wealthy or luxurious, and 'Affluent' means prosperous, which don't fit in this context. 'Advent' is a noun that correctly describes the arrival of the monsoon, which is the central event being discussed.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The southwest monsoon stormed into Kerala, a week ahead of its scheduled arrival on June 1. This was not a surprise because as early as the first week of May, meteorologists had ____A_____ the country about its early arrival over the Andaman Sea — a crucial pit stop before its _____B_______ over Kerala. A May onset is not an exceptional occurrence — it has happened four times _______C______2014 — but additional support from a pre-cyclonic circulation in the Arabian Sea gave it a _______D_______ surge due to which several parts of Maharashtra and the Konkan coast saw the monsoon reach their shores earlier than anticipated. It was the first time in 35 years that Mumbai, which usually welcomes the monsoon around June 10, saw a May onset. Even prior to the onset, the India Meteorological Department (IMD), as far back as mid-April, had forecast an ‘above normal’ monsoon rainfall, or 5% ______E______ the historical average of 87 cm. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3
questions.explanation: C) 'Since' का use होगा क्योंकि "since" का अर्थ होता है किसी निश्चित समय से अब तक। sentence में mention किया गया है कि यह घटना 2014 से चार बार हुई है, इसलिए 'since' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'From' का अर्थ है किसी समय से, 'For' का अर्थ है किसी अवधि तक, और 'By' का अर्थ है किसी समय तक, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'Since' will be used because it means from a specific time until now. The sentence mentions that this event has happened four times since 2014, making 'since' fitting here. Whereas, 'From' means starting at a time, 'For' means for a duration, and 'By' means until a time, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The southwest monsoon stormed into Kerala, a week ahead of its scheduled arrival on June 1. This was not a surprise because as early as the first week of May, meteorologists had ____A_____ the country about its early arrival over the Andaman Sea — a crucial pit stop before its _____B_______ over Kerala. A May onset is not an exceptional occurrence — it has happened four times _______C______2014 — but additional support from a pre-cyclonic circulation in the Arabian Sea gave it a _______D_______ surge due to which several parts of Maharashtra and the Konkan coast saw the monsoon reach their shores earlier than anticipated. It was the first time in 35 years that Mumbai, which usually welcomes the monsoon around June 10, saw a May onset. Even prior to the onset, the India Meteorological Department (IMD), as far back as mid-April, had forecast an ‘above normal’ monsoon rainfall, or 5% ______E______ the historical average of 87 cm. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4
questions.explanation: B) 'Tremendous' का use होगा क्योंकि "tremendous" का अर्थ होता है बहुत बड़ा या शक्तिशाली। passage में mention किया गया है कि अरब सागर में एक pre-cyclonic circulation ने मानसून को एकजबरदस्त (tremendous)गति दी, जिससे महाराष्ट्र और कोंकण तट पर मानसून समय से पहले पहुँच गया। इसलिए 'tremendous' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Advantageous' का अर्थ है फायदेमंद, 'Deleterious' का अर्थ है हानिकारक, और 'Curious' का अर्थ है जिज्ञासु, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'Tremendous' will be used because it means very large or powerful. The passage mentions that a pre-cyclonic circulation in the Arabian Sea gave the monsoon atremendoussurge, causing it to reach Maharashtra and the Konkan coast earlier than expected. Hence, 'tremendous' fits here. Whereas, 'Advantageous' means beneficial, 'Deleterious' means harmful, and 'Curious' means inquisitive, which do not fit in this context. Tremendous' is an adjective modifying the noun 'surge,' which is grammatically correct.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The southwest monsoon stormed into Kerala, a week ahead of its scheduled arrival on June 1. This was not a surprise because as early as the first week of May, meteorologists had ____A_____ the country about its early arrival over the Andaman Sea — a crucial pit stop before its _____B_______ over Kerala. A May onset is not an exceptional occurrence — it has happened four times _______C______2014 — but additional support from a pre-cyclonic circulation in the Arabian Sea gave it a _______D_______ surge due to which several parts of Maharashtra and the Konkan coast saw the monsoon reach their shores earlier than anticipated. It was the first time in 35 years that Mumbai, which usually welcomes the monsoon around June 10, saw a May onset. Even prior to the onset, the India Meteorological Department (IMD), as far back as mid-April, had forecast an ‘above normal’ monsoon rainfall, or 5% ______E______ the historical average of 87 cm. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5
questions.explanation: A) 'More than' का use होगा क्योंकि "more than" का अर्थ होता है किसी संख्या या मात्रा से अधिक। sentence में mention किया गया है कि यह घटना 2014 के बाद चार बार हुई है, इसलिए 'more than' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'much' का अर्थ है बहुत (केवल uncountable nouns के साथ), 'more' का अर्थ है अधिक (परंतु 'than' के बिना अधूरा है), और 'much than' grammatically incorrect है। 'More than' will be used because it means exceeding a number or quantity. The sentence mentions that this event has happened four times since 2014, making 'more than' appropriate here. Whereas, 'much' means a large amount (only for uncountable nouns), 'more' means greater (but incomplete without 'than'), and 'much than' is grammatically incorrect.
